_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: LLM-Assisted Merge Conflict Resolution (Phase 0 plumbing + Phase 1 dry-run)

steps.description: Pipeline: new executor/auto_merge.toml (default phase=0) + edits to executor/worktree_commit.sh to classify conflict files against refuse-list/allowlist and emit AUTO_RESOLVE_REQUESTED / AUTO_RESOLVE_SKIPPED stdout markers. Add --resume-rebase flag stub (refuses in Phase 1).
Per-agent review of S01: marker schema, refuse-list completeness, stdout/stderr separation, phase=0 default safe-by-construction.

Backend: new orch/daemon/auto_merge.py (TOML loader, classifier, prompt builder, LLM invocation via step_executor.sh, 4 new DaemonEvent types) + edits to orch/daemon/merge_queue.py (marker parsing + dispatch + safety try/except). Minimal extension to executor/step_executor_lib.sh for the auto_merge_resolve step type.
Per-agent review of S03: phase=0 short-circuit, dry-run never-applies invariant, refuse-list precedence, prompt-builder determinism, agent_runtime_options lookup, event ordering, metadata size cap.
Cross-agent final review of S01..S04: bash↔Python marker round-trip, phase=0 default behaviour, refuse-list defence-in-depth, operator UX preservation, decision-tree consistency.

Tests: unit tests for config/classifier/prompt/marker; fixture-based integration tests for AC1 (I-00085 shape), AC2 (I-00086 shape), AC3 (refuse-list), AC4 (operator UX), AC5 (phase 0), AC6 (hot reload); Invariant 3/5/8 coverage; FakeLLM mock at Python boundary.
Per-agent review of S06 tests: AC coverage, Invariant coverage, assertion strength, isolation rules, LLM-mock hygiene, no real LLM calls in CI.

Final cross-agent review including tests: implementation × tests round-trip, AC/Invariant final sign-off, holistic safety (phase 0 provably no-op, phase 1 provably non-destructive), operator UX preservation, Phase 0 production-readiness.
Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill (focus on dry-run discipline invariants and bash↔Python marker round-trip).
